# Broadside

> A Python framework for parallel LLM agent orchestration using scatter/gather instead of hierarchy.

Broadside implements the scatter/gather pattern as its core organizing principle. A task is scattered to N parallel agents (default 3, sweet spot 3-5), results are gathered, and a synthesis step produces a single output. No inter-agent communication, no hierarchy, no coordination overhead.

## Core concepts

- Task: a tightly scoped unit of work with a clear output
- Scatter: fan a task to N parallel agents (performance plateaus beyond 4-5, DeepMind 2025)
- Gather: collect and normalize outputs
- Synthesize: collapse N outputs into one — three strategies:
  - `llm` (default): general-purpose, sends all outputs to a model for unified synthesis
  - `consensus`: best for knowledge tasks, extracts agreements and disagreements
  - `voting`: best for reasoning tasks, majority-wins
- Budget: per-scatter token limits (scatter/gather multiplies costs ~15x; synthesis adds more)

## Backends

- Ollama (default, no API key needed, free cloud tier with sign-in)
  - Default model: nemotron-3-super:cloud (runs on Ollama's cloud)
  - All models run in parallel by default; use --sequential if hardware can't handle it
- Anthropic (optional extra, `pip install broadside[anthropic]`)
- OpenAI-compatible (optional extra, `pip install broadside[openai]`)

## Quick start

```bash
pip install broadside
python -m broadside run --prompt "Your task here" --n 3
```

```python
from broadside import Task, run_sync

task = Task(prompt="Your task here")
result = run_sync(task, n=3, backend="ollama")
print(result.result)
```

Results are saved to `broadside_output/{model}/{topic}_{timestamp}/`.

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/jsesler/broadside
- Docs: https://jsesler.github.io/broadside
- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/broadside/
